_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (va-legislature-refine runbook)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 Alaska House members (HD29-33): Garret Nelson (R), Kevin McCabe (R), Maxine Dibert (D), Will Stapp (R), Mike Prax (R). State tier. Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. Cross-party catches: Dibert (D) PP-endorsed pro-choice; Prax (R) opposed a stricter pro-life amendment on HB195; McCabe (R) defends tribal voter ID + ballot curing despite leading RCV repeal; Stapp now in GOP minority (was coalition in his first term).

profile.background: Sutton/Mat-Su Borough Republican; legal name Gerald 'Garret' Nelson. APPOINTED to AK House Dist. 29 by Gov. Dunleavy Dec 24, 2025 (sworn in Dec 30, 2025) to replace George Rauscher, who moved to the state Senate (~6-month record). Cattle rancher; homeschools his nine children. GOP minority caucus; committees: Corrections, Transportation, Public Safety, Community & Regional Affairs, Transportation.

garret-nelson.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). ROSTER CONFIRMED: appointed Dec 2025; sitting HD-29 incumbent seeking a full term in 2026 (primary Aug 18, 2026; field per Wikipedia includes Nelson plus Menard (R), Stone (D), Hansen (I), Kusner-Kalarchik (I), Frisby (L)) -- not withdrawn/retiring. THIN RECORD: a ~6-month appointed freshman with no candidate questionnaire, issue-group rating, or marquee culture-war vote located; the well-known April 2025 abortion/trans budget amendments predate his tenure. Self-describes as an 'unashamed conservative'; favors statutory-formula PFD, resource development, and fiscal restraint. Only defensible cells: homeschool-freedom (homeschools all nine children) and fiscal discipline (own words). All other categories: no public position found -> null (not inferred from party).

0.note: Homeschools all nine of his children full-time -- a personal commitment to homeschool freedom (SOFT; personal practice, no homeschool-policy vote located).

2.note: Own words (freshmen profile): families 'shouldn't spend more than they earn'; opposes drawing down the Permanent Fund as a long-term budget fix. Documented fiscal-restraint position (SOFT).

profile.background: Big Lake/Mat-Su Republican; retired U.S. Coast Guard chief petty officer (1976-1996) and retired Atlas Air Boeing 747 pilot; b. Hibbing, MN; Embry-Riddle BS, Regis MBA. Alaska House since 2021 (Dist. 8, then Dist. 30 after redistricting). Vocal on energy, the PFD, guns, life, and ranked-choice-voting repeal.

kevin-mccabe.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). CANDIDACY CURRENT: sitting HD-30 incumbent, active 2026 campaign (votekevinmccabe.org carries 2026 branding); AK primary Aug 18, 2026. Strong HARD record: prime-sponsored HB107 (personhood/life-at-conception); iVoterGuide survey affirms one-man/one-woman marriage, opposes 'gender ideology, grooming, bodily mutilation,' backs 'dollars follow the child'/ESAs/homeschool, 'BUILD THE WALL'/deportation, opposes ESG for public funds; op-ed opposing red-flag HB89 + safe-storage HB134; 'Pastors we need you / Black-Robed Regiment' op-ed. DEVIATION (election integrity): though he LEADS the RCV-repeal push, his SB64 Substack defends tribal ID cards as valid voter ID and supports absentee-ballot curing -- moderate vs. the purist photo-ID/anti-mail rubric, so those cells are null, not true. Cells with no found evidence (abortion abolition, IVF/stem-cell, euthanasia, civil unions, no-fault divorce, porn age-verify, faith adoption, CBDC/sound-money, all of public_justice and refuse_federal_overreach, constitutional carry, NFA repeal, castle doctrine) left null.

0.note: HARD: prime-sponsored HB107 to define personhood/life as beginning at conception; 'the human zygote... is a totally separate life.'
HARD: iVoterGuide survey -- 'Marriage is a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman.'

2.note: HARD: opposes taxpayer-funded gender transition; supports 'protecting children from gender ideology, grooming, and bodily mutilation' (iVoterGuide).

4.note: HARD: opposes promotion of gender ideology in schools/policy (iVoterGuide).

0.note: HARD: backs 'dollars to follow the child,' Education Savings Accounts, charter expansion, and homeschool freedom (iVoterGuide + 2025 op-ed).

2.note: HARD: opposes gender-ideology curricula and 'grooming' in schools (iVoterGuide).

0.note: HARD: authored 'Pastors we need you. Where is today's Black-Robed Regiment?' -- public call for Christians/pastors to profess faith in the public square.

1.note: HARD: strongly disagrees businesses should be forced to provide services violating religious beliefs (conscience exemption) (iVoterGuide).

3.note: HARD: government should 'Never' close churches (iVoterGuide) -- protects public Christian worship.

4.note: HARD: opposes ESG investment criteria for public funds (iVoterGuide).

1.note: HARD: 'Illegal immigration should be met with deportation' (iVoterGuide).
HARD: publicly opposed red-flag HB89 ('firearm confiscation without due process') and safe-storage HB134 (2025 op-ed); 'The Second Amendment is not conditional.'

profile.background: Fairbanks Democrat; Koyukon Athabascan; 21-year Denali Elementary public-school teacher. AK House Dist. 31 since Jan 2023; re-elected Nov 2024 (54.2% over R Bart LeBon). House Majority Coalition; committees: Education, Resources, Armed Services, Tribal Affairs. Term ends Jan 2027.

maxine-dibert.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). Sitting HD-31 incumbent; 2026 re-filing not yet confirmable at time of writing (AK filing/DOE list pending) but no evidence of withdrawal/retirement. CROSS-PARTY CHECK (the high-value catch for a Democrat) came back NEGATIVE: hunted for a rural-Alaska-Native pro-gun or pro-life deviation and found NONE documented. CAUTION: a search summary falsely implied she authored a 2025 red-flag bill -- the actual sponsors of HB 89 (Gun Violence Protective Orders) are Josephson/Hannan/Mina/Gray; Dibert is NOT a listed sponsor, so guns left null, not scored. Documented progressive positions: endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund ('Repro Champs') and a self-described 'fierce advocate for reproductive health care' who pledged to protect Alaska's constitutional abortion right -> scored FALSE on abortion-abolition and abortion-industry-funding cells. Most cells (immigration, CBDC, NFA, federalism, justice) null -- no cited position either way.

1.note: DEVIATION (documented contrary): Planned Parenthood Action Fund 'Repro Champs' endorsee; self-described 'fierce advocate for reproductive health care' who pledged to protect Alaska's constitutional abortion right. Documented pro-abortion-rights advocacy -- contrary to abortion abolition/restriction.

4.note: Planned Parenthood Action Fund 'Repro Champs' endorsee -- carries abortion-industry political backing.

profile.background: East Fairbanks / Fort Wainwright Republican. AK House Dist. 32 since Jan 2023; reelected Nov 2024 (67.3%). In the 34th Legislature (2025-26) a leading member of the 19-member Republican MINORITY caucus on House Finance (was in the bipartisan majority coalition only in his first 2023-24 term). Term ends Jan 2027.

will-stapp.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). CANDIDACY CONFIRMED: active 2026 incumbent -- Republican Seth Church filed to challenge him for the East Fairbanks seat (ADN, June 1 2026 filing deadline), so not withdrawn/retiring. ROSTER CORRECTION: now in the GOP minority caucus, NOT the bipartisan majority -- his current record is mainstream-GOP-minority, not 'moderate coalition.' THIN SOCIAL RECORD: a fiscal/resource/energy-focused legislator with little public position-taking on the life/marriage/guns/elections categories. Defensible cells: fiscal discipline (platform 'Fiscal Responsibility... Protecting the Permanent Fund' + voted with the GOP minority against the majority operating budget) and pro-law-enforcement (campaign 'Safe Neighborhoods... collaboration with law enforcement'). A bare 'pro-life' self-label without a conception statement or life vote was held to the high bar -> sanctity_of_life left null. All other categories: no public position found -> null.

2.note: Platform: 'Fiscal Responsibility and Accountability in Government Spending' / 'Protecting the Permanent Fund'; voted with the GOP minority against the majority's draft operating budget on fiscal-discipline grounds. Documented opposition to over-spending.

0.note: Campaign plank (own words): 'Safe Neighborhoods... collaboration with law enforcement.' Affirmative pro-law-enforcement priority (SOFT).

profile.background: North Pole / Badger Road area Republican; legal name Glenn M. 'Mike' Prax. Appointed Feb 2020 (filled Tammie Wilson's vacancy), then elected 2020, 2022, and 2024 (won 96.8%, unopposed). Chaired the House Health & Social Services Committee (2023-24). CPAC lifetime rating 72%. Term ends Jan 2027.

mike-prax.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06-17 (state tier). Sitting HD-33 incumbent; has run unopposed each cycle; 2026 re-filing not definitively confirmable at time of writing (AK DOE list pending) but no evidence of withdrawal/retirement. LIFE is a documented SPLIT: in a candidate Q&A he affirmed human life begins at conception/fertilization and the unborn deserve the same legal protections as infants (pro-life on personhood) -- BUT on HB 195 (2026, pharmacist prescribing) he OPPOSED Rep. Jamie Allard's stricter anti-abortion-drug amendment, arguing broad language could block miscarriage treatment ('I learned way more than I ever wanted to about abortions'). That moderate posture drew pro-life pushback and is scored as a documented deviation. CPAC 72% lifetime = mid-tier, not a hardliner. Despite his Health-committee chairmanship, no citable COVID-vaccine-mandate / informed-consent vote located -> industry_capture null. Marriage, family, christian liberty, elections, immigration, guns, justice, federalism: no public position found -> null.

0.note: HARD (own words, candidate Q&A): concurs with the American College of Pediatricians that 'human life begins at conception -- fertilization,' and the unborn 'deserve the same legal protections we already afford to infants.'

1.note: DEVIATION (documented contrary): on HB 195 (2026) he opposed/argued down Rep. Jamie Allard's stricter anti-abortion-drug amendment, prioritizing miscarriage-care access -- a moderate posture that drew pro-life criticism. Contrary to abortion abolition.
